1: A revelation of God: the question "from where does my help come?" is the question the psalm immediately answers — from YHWH the creator. In the NT, the creator-helper has entered his creation: John 1:3,14 — "all things were made through him... the Word became flesh." Christ is the help that comes "from the LORD" in the most literal sense — the divine helper incarnate.
2: A revelation of God: "My help comes from the LORD, who made heaven and earth." Colossians 1:16-17 identifies Christ as the one through whom all things in heaven and earth were created and in whom all things hold together. The maker of heaven and earth who is the help of Israel is the Christ through whom that creation was made. Hebrews 2:18 — because he himself suffered, "he is able to help those who are being tempted."
3: A shadow: the keeper who prevents the foot from slipping. John 10:28-29 — "I give them eternal life, and they shall never perish; no one will snatch them out of my hand." Christ is the keeper who holds his people so securely that their feet cannot ultimately be moved. 1 Peter 1:5 — believers "are guarded through faith for a salvation ready to be revealed."
4: A shadow: "He who keeps Israel will neither slumber nor sleep." The sleepless guardian finds its NT fulfillment in Christ, the permanent intercessor. Hebrews 7:25 — "he always lives to make intercession for them." Never off duty, never asleep — Christ prays for his people continuously and permanently from his place at the Father's right hand (Romans 8:34).
5: A theme: "the LORD is your shade on your right hand." The imagery of protective shade points toward Christ as the one in whom believers find shelter. John 10:9 — "I am the door; whoever enters by me will be saved and will go in and out and find pasture." Matthew 23:37 — Christ longed to gather Jerusalem "as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings" — the shade and cover of his own person.
6: A theme: protection from the elements — sun and moon. The total, comprehensive protection described is the protection believers have in Christ. Romans 8:38-39 — nothing in all creation shall separate us from the love of God in Christ Jesus. Revelation 7:16 — those before the throne "shall hunger no more, neither thirst anymore; the sun shall not strike them, nor any scorching heat."
7: A shadow: "He will keep your going out and your coming in." The Good Shepherd who "goes before" his sheep (John 10:3-4), who calls them by name and leads them — Christ is the guardian of every movement of his people. 1 Peter 2:25 — "you were straying like sheep, but have now returned to the Shepherd and Overseer of your souls." The keeping is eternal: "from this time forth and forevermore."
8: A shadow: everlasting keeping — "from this time forth and forevermore." John 11:25-26 — "I am the resurrection and the life. Whoever believes in me, though he die, yet shall he live, and everyone who lives and believes in me shall never die." The eternal dimension of the keeper's protection is realized in Christ's resurrection — the keeper who conquered death keeps his own for eternity.
